---
title: "D. Project Checklist"
---

# D. Project Checklist

Use this checklist before submitting an empirical project.

## Research Question

- [ ] The research question is specific.
- [ ] The dependent variable is clear.
- [ ] The main explanatory variable is clear.
- [ ] The question is feasible with available data.
- [ ] The question is economic, not only technical.

## Data Source

- [ ] The data source is identified.
- [ ] The data source can be verified.
- [ ] The collection date is recorded.
- [ ] The raw dataset is preserved.
- [ ] Any limitations are stated.

## Dataset Integrity

- [ ] The unit of observation is defined.
- [ ] Missing values are checked.
- [ ] Duplicate rows are checked.
- [ ] Outliers are checked.
- [ ] Categorical levels are checked.

## Variable Definitions

- [ ] Each variable has a clear definition.
- [ ] Units are reported where relevant.
- [ ] Constructed variables are explained.
- [ ] The dependent variable is justified.
- [ ] Control variables are justified.

## Data Cleaning

- [ ] Cleaning steps are documented.
- [ ] Raw and cleaned files are separate.
- [ ] No unexplained observations are deleted.
- [ ] Code can reproduce the cleaned dataset.
- [ ] Cleaning choices are economically reasonable.

## Descriptive Statistics

- [ ] Numeric variables have summary statistics.
- [ ] Categorical variables have frequency tables.
- [ ] Unusual values are discussed.
- [ ] Descriptive statistics match the research question.

## Graphs

- [ ] Graphs are readable.
- [ ] Axes are labeled.
- [ ] Each graph has a purpose.
- [ ] Graphs support the research question.
- [ ] Graphs are not used as causal proof.

## Econometric Model

- [ ] The regression equation is written clearly.
- [ ] Variables in the equation match the dataset.
- [ ] The model choice is justified.
- [ ] Coefficients are interpreted correctly.
- [ ] Causality claims are avoided unless the design supports them.

## Diagnostics

- [ ] Residuals are checked.
- [ ] Heteroskedasticity is considered.
- [ ] Multicollinearity is considered.
- [ ] Functional form is considered.
- [ ] Robustness checks are included where appropriate.

## Interpretation

- [ ] Results are explained in words.
- [ ] Statistical significance is reported.
- [ ] Economic significance is discussed.
- [ ] Limitations are stated.
- [ ] Conclusions follow from the evidence.

## Tables and Figures

- [ ] Tables are clean and readable.
- [ ] Figures are numbered or clearly referenced.
- [ ] Regression results include key statistics.
- [ ] Tables and figures are discussed in the text.

## Writing Quality

- [ ] The paper has a logical structure.
- [ ] Paragraphs are concise.
- [ ] Terms are used consistently.
- [ ] The paper answers one main question.
- [ ] The conclusion does not introduce new results.

## Reproducibility

- [ ] Code runs from top to bottom.
- [ ] File paths are documented.
- [ ] Packages are listed.
- [ ] Random seeds are set when needed.
- [ ] Output can be recreated.

## Final Submission

- [ ] Dataset is included or linked.
- [ ] Code file is included.
- [ ] Variable table is included.
- [ ] Results tables are included.
- [ ] Final report is complete.

## Minimum Acceptable Project Package

- dataset
- code
- variable table
- graphs
- regression results
- diagnostics
- short empirical report
